summary: J Michael Consulting supports a FHIR-enabled future for health data interoperability but urges retention of critical certification requirements, especially for data security and public health reporting. They caution against removing CDA-based document exchange requirements prematurely and emphasize the need for consistent standards to avoid interoperability issues.
specific_provisions: Transmission to Cancer Registries certification requirement
key_arguments: FHIR is key to future interoperability but CDA-based documents remain critical today for patient care and public health.
Removing certification criteria without clear standards risks variable implementations and burdens on public health and private sectors.
Security and patient data protection must remain paramount in certification requirements.
Transition from CDA to FHIR-based reporting requires realistic timelines and resource support for public health agencies.
recommendations: Retain CDA-based document exchange certification criteria until FHIR-based alternatives are fully supported and resourced.
Maintain existing security requirements until better privacy and security standards are developed.
Ensure certified HIT products maintain a consistent baseline functionality to support interoperability and a level playing field.
Incorporate requirements for FHIR Subscription and Bulk Data Access with cohort creation capabilities in certified HIT.
notable_quote: We strongly recommend that Certification Criteria retain requirements that certified HIT adhere to existing HL7 interoperability standards.
